summary: Matched and substantially improved several HUD status/damage-percent routines in src/melee/if/ifstatus.c, replacing multiple NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs and the asm-only marker for ifStatus_802F6EA4. The PR also corrected ifstatus prototypes and changed IfDamageState damage fields from unsigned to signed 16-bit to support sentinel values such as -1. Bot results reported 4 new matches in ifstatus, including ifStatus_802F4B84, ifStatus_802F5B48, ifStatus_802F6508, and ifStatus_802F6EA4, plus improvements to ifStatus_802F61FC, ifStatus_802F5EC0, ifStatus_802F4EDC, and ifStatus_802F66A4.
what_changed: Implemented ifStatus_802F4B84 as the damage digit shake routine, using frames_of_shake_remaining, damage_from_last_attack, stored digit translations, HSD_Randf-derived offsets, magnitude clamping, and final translation reset when the shake ends.

Reworked ifStatus_802F4EDC around explicit HudIndex/player lookup, digit texture animation setup, JObj/TObj animation calls, hiding tens/hundreds digits with 0x10 flags, and color interpolation for the damage percent materials.
Added static inline find_player_by_entity(HSD_GObj*) and used it in ifStatus_802F5B48 to locate the IfDamageState for a HUD parent gobj.

Implemented ifStatus_802F5B48(HSD_GObj*) as the per-HUD damage update proc, reading either remaining HP for stamina-like mode or damage percent for normal mode, clamping to 0..999, comparing old_damage, setting force_digit_shake/unk10 flags, and recording damage_from_last_attack.

Implemented ifStatus_802F5EC0(IfDamageState*, s32) to create or reuse the damage-number HUD gobj, load joints/anims, set GX link/procs, cache digit JObjs and base translations, set the percent-sign texture based on Player_GetMoreFlagsBit2, and force initial damage update/render.

Implemented ifStatus_802F61FC(IfDamageState*, s32) for player mark setup, creating/reusing a mark gobj, loading DmgMrk assets, selecting character icon animation, positioning via ifAll_802F3424, and applying player/team HUD color.

Converted the previous forward declaration of ifStatus_802F6508 into an implementation that initializes a player's HUD state, calls mark and damage HUD setup, optionally initializes related UI, and chooses stock display mode from StartMeleeRules.
Implemented ifStatus_802F66A4 to load DmgNum_scene_models and DmgMrk_scene_models from the interface archive and populate HudIndex asset pointers.
Implemented ifStatus_802F6EA4, replacing an asm placeholder marker, to either fire callbacks/audio for arg0 == 8 or create/configure an animated gobj from ifStatus_803F9628 element data.
Updated ifstatus.h prototypes for ifStatus_802F5B48, ifStatus_802F5EC0, and ifStatus_802F61FC to take the arguments used by the new C implementations.
Updated IfDamageState.damage_percent and IfDamageState.old_damage in if/types.h from u16 to s16.
smart_moves: Changing damage_percent and old_damage to s16 is supported by the implementation’s use of -1 as an initialization/sentinel value; leaving these as u16 would obscure comparisons like old_damage != -1 and signed clamping below zero.
The damage shake routine preserves original translation_x/translation_y per digit and restores them when frames_of_shake_remaining reaches 1, avoiding accumulated random offsets.

The implementation uses explicit digit enum-style indices such as Ones, Tens, Hundreds, and Percent in ifStatus_802F4EDC, making the HUD digit logic searchable and less dependent on raw numeric child positions.
The asset-loading routine centralizes DmgNum and DmgMrk archive section extraction into HudIndex fields before the setup routines consume those joint/anims/matanims/shapeanims pointers.
The PR adjusted headers at the same time as implementing call sites, preventing stale void(void) prototypes for proc functions that actually receive HSD_GObj or state/player parameters.
decomp_lessons: When a field is initialized to -1 or compared against -1 in decompiled logic, verify the struct field signedness in types.h rather than forcing casts around unsigned fields.

For HSD HUD routines, matching often requires following actual archive layout chains such as janim_selection_joints->child->child->...->aobjdesc->fobjdesc instead of introducing convenient temporary HSD_MatAnimJoint or HSD_TexAnim variables.
Per-frame GObj proc callbacks may need their real HSD_GObj* argument reflected in headers; using void prototypes can hide important matching and call-convention evidence.
Damage HUD digit rendering combines JObj animation, TObj texture frame requests, AObj rate zeroing, and JObj visibility flags; future status work should treat these as a repeated pattern.
Preserving original local structure, explicit repeated expressions, and PAD_STACK calls can be necessary for matching even when the C could be simplified.
naming_conventions: Existing project prefix style is retained: ifStatus_802Fxxxx for unresolved status functions, ifAll_802Fxxxx for shared interface helpers, ifStock_802Fxxxx for stock UI, and gm_8016xxxx for game-mode helpers.
New helper find_player_by_entity describes the lookup behavior but remains static inline and local to ifstatus.c.
The helper offset_rand is named for its behavior, returning HSD_Randf() - 0.5f, but evidence is weak that this is a final project-preferred name.
Struct fields remain partly unknown where evidence is insufficient, such as unk9, unk10, unk258, unk268, unk26C, unk270, and unk274.
Digit indices use semantic names already present in the code/diff: Ones, Tens, Hundreds, Percent.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced NOT_IMPLEMENTED in ifStatus_802F4B84, ifStatus_802F5B48, ifStatus_802F5EC0, ifStatus_802F61FC, and ifStatus_802F66A4 with C implementations.
Replaced the asm-placeholder comment /// #ifStatus_802F6EA4 with a C implementation that bot-reported as a 100% new match.
Used PAD_STACK(8), PAD_STACK(0x18), and PAD_STACK(0x10) in implemented functions to satisfy stack layout/matching constraints.
Kept explicit casts to HSD_AnimJoint**, HSD_MatAnimJoint**, HSD_ShapeAnimJoint**, HSD_TexAnim*, and proc callback signatures around lb_8000C07C, GObj_SetupGXLink, and HSD_GObj_SetupProc.

Used repeated direct dereferences such as digit_jobj->u.dobj->mobj->tobj and state->jobjs[...] rather than abstracting all digit update logic, likely preserving instruction order for ifStatus_802F4EDC matching.
Used magic constants visible in the diff, including 0.1014f, 1.5207f, 0.2028f for shake magnitude/offsets; 0x3E7 for damage clamp; 0x10 for digit hide flags; and 0xE/0xF/0xB/0x11 for GObj setup.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp-dev bot reported matched code improved by +0.04% / +1664 bytes for GALE01.
Bot reported 4 new matches: ifStatus_802F4B84 (+851 bytes), ifStatus_802F5B48 (+659 bytes), ifStatus_802F6EA4 (+400 bytes), and ifStatus_802F6508 (+340 bytes), all to 100.00%.
Bot reported improvements in unmatched ifstatus items: ifStatus_802F61FC to 96.36%, ifStatus_802F5EC0 to 95.20%, ifStatus_802F4EDC to 80.38%, and ifStatus_802F66A4 to 98.25%.

Bot also reported two broken matches/regressions outside or adjacent to the main work: mnCharSel_8026688C_OnEnter from 100.00% to 99.90%, ifStatus_802F7134 from 100.00% to 99.98%, and an ifstatus .sdata2 unmatched regression.
follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs touching ifStatus_802F4EDC or ifStatus_802F7134 to see whether the bot-reported broken match was recovered.
Search for renames or struct refinements for IfDamageState fields unk9, unk10, unk258, unk268, unk26C, unk270, and unk274.
Compare future damage HUD work for reuse of the signed damage_percent/old_damage sentinel pattern.
Check whether offset_rand or find_player_by_entity were later renamed or inlined differently for style/matching reasons.
Investigate why this PR affected mncharsel matching metrics despite only changing ifstatus-related files in the slice.
